title: Paper 1 — The LCR Kernel and Chart: A Unified Foundation
abstract: The LCR kernel is a finite-state machine on the 3-cube {0,1}³, with bits named L (left boundary), C (center), R (right boundary). We establish that the LCR carrier is the minimal ordered local carrier preserving one distinguished center while allowing two opposed boundary directions — requiring exactly three ordered positions. The kernel carries a ℤ/4 shell grading with binomial distribution (1, 3, 3, 1), admits a reversal involution with 4 fixed points and 2 swap pairs, and is in bijection with the 8-element subset of J₃(𝕆) consisting of binary diagonal matrices. We prove the shell profile, reversal involution, chart–J₃(𝕆) bijection, trace preservation, and substrate map preservation at depth 4096. We derive the VOA partition Z(q) = 2q⁰ + 6q⁵, the static ℤ₄ period template (2 fixed + 6 period-4), and the Gluon invariance Γ(s) = C. All claims are backed by receipts and verified by 12,561 machine checks with 0 defects.

D_claims.claim: Minimal LCR Carrier theorem: any ordered local carrier preserving one distinguished center and two addressable boundaries requires at least three positions
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 4.1 — minimal carrier proof by address counting

D_claims.claim: Minimality of 8-state space: Σ = {0,1}³ is unique minimal basis supporting full S₃ action, correction boundary ∂, and VOA partition
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 4.2 — T3 Isomorphism 6,272 checks / 0 defects at 4,096 depths
Theorem 5.1 — Hamming weight binomial count on {0,1}³

D_claims.claim: Reversal involution σ(L,C,R) = (R,C,L) is an involution with 4 fixed points and 2 swap pairs
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 6.1 — direct enumeration and orbit verification

D_claims.claim: Chart–J₃(𝕆) bijection φ(L,C,R) = diag(L,C,R) is a bijection between Σ and J₃(𝕆)diag^{{0,1}}
Trace preservation: tr_J₃(𝕆)(φ(L,C,R)) = L + C + R = shell
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 7.2 — trace equals sum of diagonal entries

D_claims.claim: Substrate map reproduces canonical Rule 30 evolution at depth 4096 with 0 defects
VOA partition Z(q) = 2q⁰ + 6q⁵ (2 vacua weight 0, 6 excited weight 5)
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 9.1 — centroid VOA construction verified by verify_voa_partition (4/4 PASS)

D_claims.claim: ℤ₄ period template: 2 fixed points + 6 period-4 states, no period-2
Gluon invariance Γ(s) = C = Γ(σ(s)) for all 8/8 states; 64/64 invariant observer rows
Shell-2 chiral doublet {(1,1,0), (0,1,1)} exchanged by reversal with pivot (1,0,1) fixed
O8 spinor double-cover closure: F² = −1 at 2π and F⁴ = +1 at 4π
12,561 total checks with 0 defects and 100% PASS

I_claims.claim: The 3-bit choice is forced by SU(3) triality requirements
I_claims.reasoning: Structural reading: 2-bit gives trivial grading (1,2,1); 4-bit gives (1,4,6,4,1) lacking 3+3 structure

I_claims.claim: Reversal σ is the Weyl (1,3) transposition in the S₃ Weyl group of A₂ = SU(3)
I_claims.reasoning: Structural correspondence between reversal and Weyl group

I_claims.claim: The 8 states are objects of the 2-category ℒ at the carrier level (Paper 80)
The 3 trace-2 idempotents correspond to 3 fermion generations (Papers 41–44)
I_claims.reasoning: Downstream structural mapping, not proven in this paper

I_claims.claim: The shell-2 doublet and correction doublet are distinct but related with intersection (1,1,0)
I_claims.reasoning: Structural reading of the two frameworks
